problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: Stem cell differentiation into target tissue type
hypothesis: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Stem. Stem cell differentiation into target tissue type directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Mouse model knockout study with 26 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.
